rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope in AGREE annex blocks
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Preserve the original AADL structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope in AGREE annex blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the timing properties
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
